mappings.rationale: Mormonism is listed as a sect tangentially related to Christianity in the parent section (827.H.3), but also represents a new religious movement reflecting the impact of dominant cultures, relevant to 829.A.
Covers the foundational scripture of Mormonism, a new religious movement relevant to 829.A.
Directly covers Rastafari as a new religious movement reflecting the impact of dominant cultures on African diaspora peoples, relevant to 829.A and 829.B.
Covers African American religious movements including the Nation of Islam and Black Jewish cults, relevant to 829.B on Negro cults in Western cultures.
Covers modern pagan revivals and neo-paganism, directly relevant to 829.F on residues or revivals of ancient and primitive religious beliefs in modern civilizations.
Covers modern witchcraft movements including Wicca, directly relevant to 829.F.1 on witchcraft and black magic in modern civilizations.
Covers modern magical practices and beliefs, relevant to 829.F.1 on black magic in modern civilizations.
Covers modern superstitious beliefs and practices, relevant to 829.F on residues of ancient beliefs in modern civilizations.
Pentecostalism represents a newer charismatic movement reflecting evolving religious culture, relevant to 829.A on new religious movements reflecting the impact of dominant cultures.
Evangelicalism includes many newer movements and churches, relevant to 829.A on new religious movements reflecting dominant cultures.
Covers fundamentalist movements across religions as newer religious responses to modernity, relevant to 829.A.
Covers modern Islamic movements and their global spread, relevant to 829.E on religions and religious movements of Islamic origin.
Freemasonry is a fraternal movement with quasi-religious elements and theosophical connections, relevant to 829.C on theosophical groups.
Covers the diverse new religious movements in America including those listed in 829.A-B such as the Nation of Islam, Mormonism, and Christian Science.
Covers modern spiritual movements outside traditional religion, relevant to 829.D on spiritualist groups and 829.F.3 on healing cults.
Covers the secular context against which new religious movements emerge, providing background for understanding 829.A.
Provides context for understanding new religious movements within the broader study of religion.
Modern druidism represents a revival of ancient religious practices, relevant to 829.F on residues or revivals of ancient beliefs in modern civilizations.
